comments: Originally it was thought the Taurus session was Tx on this show, we now know first broadcast was 06 June 1980
Tony Wilson, "Two broken feet and he still won't give me any time off!"

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in studio on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Oh hello there, Thomas the Vance here, TV on the radio, and welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
Tonight, a repeat of the set that was put out from the stage at Reading last year by the USA-based UK group Climax Blues Band,
who've been together now for 12 years, but still manage to stay nice and fresh.
we've got new releases by iron maiden brand x stingray motorhead zon wild horses the scorpions
and 38 special classic cuts by alice cooper and super trying who super tramp thank you among
others not forgetting our friday night connection and a little titillation comes into your life
this evening we start with this leonard skinner and freebird especially for all the lads in the

exeter holiday camp that's stick and dewey and brad and mitch and pete and dick and ivor and
Ditch and Phil.
And also for John, who lives in Whitehaven in Cumbria.
And for Simon Carr, who lives in Eton in Windsor.
For a couple of people who are on the 1st of May going to go over to Johannesburg,
good luck to you, John and Linda Rogers.
Not forgetting a gentleman by the name of Adrian Thomas, who lives in Port Talbot.
And Arv and Arnie and Sprin from Telford.
And also Mike and Tom and Annabelle and John, who live in Westfield in Hastings.
And for a lovely lady by the name of Leslie McEwan,
who lives at the Manor Hospital Nurses' Home in Walsall in Staffordshire,
wants me to mention Tony and Peter and Tony,
Ozzy Plant, Kenny, Paul, Paul, Charlie,
and all the other long-haired, denim-clad rock freaks.
Not forgetting her mum, Maureen.
And here's something new from Iron Maiden.
That was Magnum off their album Marauder,

which is just about to come out on Jet Records.
The track you heard was In The Beginning.
And before that, you heard something from Iron Maiden
from their eponymous album,
And the track was Phantom of the Opera, and that's a really tasty album.
It's out on EMI.
And I'd like to dedicate that to a gentleman by the name of Mark Smith.
It's his 17th birthday tomorrow, the 19th of April.
He lives at [redacted address], Erdington, in Birmingham.
And Steve, send your best wishes, Mark.
And I'd like to thank you, Steve, for this remarkably beautiful postcard
of that wonderful British site, the Bullring in Birmingham.
Also dedicate those couple of tracks to everybody in the fourth year,
all the heavy metal freaks at cleave comprehensive school in gloucestershire 10 27 and here's

and Stratford-on-Avon, a nice part of the world.

And also for Jazz Brock of [redacted address] in Edinburgh.
And for everybody who lives at [redacted address],
Durbin Field, I think it possibly is, in Cheshire.
That's Alexander Dashak.
And not forgetting Jez and Pete and Mick and Larry and Ev
and Loner and Midge and Dave and Kaz and Tone and Bob and Rat and Kitten.
And Mick Lee, who wants me to say hello to everybody at the Hungry Years.
A track before that was from the Alice Cooper Show.
It's just come out on Warner Brothers.

And that was for everybody at the Warrington Art College, I think it is,
in and around Halewood in Liverpool.
It's exactly a quarter to 11 and it's time for the Friday Night Connection.
The tracks we played in last work, week, work, week, work, work, week,
were Lay Lady Lay by Bob Dylan from the album Nashville Skyline, Venus,
by television from Marky Moon,
and Tarot Woman from Richie Blackmore's Rainbow.
Not a lot of answers.

And the answer was Nashville Skyline, the Nashville, Marquee Moon, the Marquee, Blackmoor's Rainbow, the Rainbow, all London venues.

it's very melodic do not change channels this is not radio three cranfield and bedfordshire

London, W1A4WW.

from phil p.s could you tell my wife kathy i love her very much no phil i won't you do it right now
That'll be just it.
Climax Blues Band, as we recorded them at Reading way back last year.

and nowadays spend most of their time in America,
but they came over last year to the Reading Festival and knocked everybody out.
The Climax Blues Band.
Tasty, tasty people.

and it's an exclusive preview of his double live album
that's coming up soon called Just One Night.
That's next week.

and the track was Turn It On.

The track was Falling In Love.
And I'm off on Monday to Paris, believe it or not,
to see that band, and also I'll be chatting to them,
and that will be on Rock On Saturday,
not tomorrow, but the Saturday following.
Tomorrow on Rock On Saturday,
B.B. King, ZZ Top, Gary Newman, and Good Music.
Four to 7.30.
Now, here's the sad news.
Unfortunately, Tony Wilson can't talk to you tonight
because he's fractured one ankle
and sprained the other on separate days.
So he can't talk, but he can say something, can't you?
Two broken feet, and he still won't give me any time, Ed.
On two separate days, I kid you not.
What, I'll lie to you?
Wherever you are, take care of yourselves.
Thanks to our engineering staff tonight here in Broadcasting House.
I'm Tommy Vance. I'll see you at four o'clock.
Good night, and God bless.
